title: Insect bites and stings
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Most insect bites and stings can be treated at home when there is no severe reaction.
Common symptoms include pain, redness, swelling, itching, burning, numbness, or tingling.
Home care may include washing the site, cold packs, itch treatment, and watching for infection.
Emergency care is needed for trouble breathing, wheezing, face or mouth swelling, throat tightness, trouble swallowing, weakness, or blue color.
